Long ago, the sky was a blanket of unbroken darkness, cold and devoid of wonder. In this endless night lived Vael, the Great Frost Fox, whose fur was spun from pure starlight. Vael was a solitary creature, wandering the frozen tundras of the upper world. One evening, he discovered a celestial ember—a fragment of the sun that had broken off and fallen into the snow.

Curious, Vael picked up the ember in his jaws. It did not burn him; instead, it filled him with an overwhelming energy. He began to run. Faster and faster he sprinted across the horizon, his paws striking the icy firmament. As he dashed, the ember's magic reacted with the frost on his coat, scattering brilliant ribbons of green, violet, and blue across the black sky.

Whenever Vael grew tired and slept, the sky would return to its dark, dormant state. But when the winter winds howled, it was said to be Vael’s joyous bark, signaling his return to the endless chase. To this day, the people of the frozen lands look up and smile when the heavens glow, knowing the Great Frost Fox is racing across the night, painting the dark with his luminous dance.

Long ago, before the world knew the chill of winter, the forests were a perpetual, vibrant green. Sylas, the spirit of the woods, tended to every leaf with eternal devotion. High above, Ignis, the restless spirit of fire, watched him. Ignis grew enamored with the gentle sway of the canopy and descended from the sun to dance among the branches.

At first, their union was beautiful. Sylas welcomed the warmth, and the trees flourished. But Ignis could not contain his fervent nature. As he embraced the leaves, his fiery passion began to scorch them, turning their emerald edges to brilliant shades of crimson, gold, and burnt orange.

Realizing his touch was destroying the very thing he loved, Ignis wept tears of ash and retreated to the sky, pulling his warmth away. Without him, a deep, bitter cold settled over the world. The stunning, fire-kissed leaves, now brittle and fragile, detached from their branches and drifted to the ground. 

To this day, the trees remember this tragic romance. Every year, they burst into fiery colors in memory of Ignis, only to let their leaves fall as they brace for the cold sorrow of his absence.

Myth: Long ago, the world’s rivers ran cold, heavy, and forever bound to the earth. Maris, the spirit of the waters, wove her currents in perpetual quiet, never knowing the touch of the sky. High above, Solis, the golden spirit of the sun, watched her shimmering surface with intense longing. Captivated by Maris's gentle ripples, Solis descended, determined to embrace the beautiful waters.

When Solis’s radiant heat met Maris’s cool surface, the reaction was immediate. His fiery warmth began to dissolve her earthly form. Maris, feeling lighter than ever before, transformed into a delicate, silver vapor. She rose upward, escaping the riverbanks and floating into the sky, entwining with Solis’s golden rays in a breathtaking dance of light and moisture. 

But Solis realized that prolonged contact would dry her up entirely, erasing her from the world. Heartbroken, he pulled back, allowing the cool night to return. Maris, heavy with sorrow, condensed and wept back down to the earth as morning dew. 

To this day, the morning mist rises as Maris reaches for her fiery lover, only to fall back as dew when Solis retreats to save her from his own burning passion.

Today, whenever a shooting star crosses the night, it is Lyra sending a piece of her heart to her beloved Frost Fox.

Above him danced Aeria, the restless spirit of the winds. Captivated by his steadfast strength, Aeria descended, wrapping her chaotic, swirling currents around his jagged peaks in an embrace of furious devotion.

Realizing her fierce love was slowly destroying the steadfast giant she adored, Aeria wept. Heartbroken, she retreated to the upper atmosphere, calming her violent storms into gentle breezes. She refused to shatter him further. 

To this day, the high peaks are softened, worn down by that ancient romance. Whenever low clouds and gentle fog drape over the mountain summits, it is Aeria returning for a fleeting, delicate kiss—loving Terran softly as mist, so she never has to break him again.
